creator: bobf@mrp3.com
text:
I've seen a similar problem on 8-STABLE (amd64 build).  The problem 
appears to be a cast of a 64-bit pointer to a 32-bit integer, which is 
now an ERROR with the current C compiler (perhaps earlier it wasn't but 
it SHOULD be since that could cause crashes).

I deinstalled ekiga, opal, and pwlib, then re-installed ekiga3 (which 
brought in opal3 and ptlib26) which all built ok (I have not tried 
running them but don't expect any problems).  gnome dependencies seem to 
handle this ok.

I recommend marking opal and ekiga as BROKEN with notes in UPDATING to 
reflect what I did.  Be advised that opal and pwlib MUST be deinstalled 
before you can install opal3 and ptlib26 (probably the same with ekiga 
and ekiga3 but I didn't verify this).
